segments.text:
Know this structure
1’ C – base attached
3’ C – free –OH group, site for attachment of next nucleotide
5’ C – phosphate group attached, NOT part of ring

Nucleic acid
phosphate pKa = 2.14
releases H+
directionality

Nitrogenous bases are stacked
one hydration shell
Base-pairing = hydrogen bonding between complementary bases

B-DNA (“normal”)
right-hand helix
10 bp/turn
smooth backbone
major and minor grooves
Z-DNA
left-hand helix
12 bp/turn
“zig-zag” backbone
only one groove

Base composition = % of DNA that is G-C basepairs
says nothing about their distribution
Base distribution = relative arrangement (organization, distribution)
of G-C basepairs throughout the DNA or genome
